heroHeadline: Trump: 'NATO WASN'T THERE WHEN WE NEEDED THEM' — Post-Rutte Meeting Truth Social Blast; 'Absolutely Considering' NATO Withdrawal
heroSubtitle: The April 8 Rutte-Trump White House summit — 2 hours, closed-door — ended without any Article 5 reassurance. Trump posted an all-caps Truth Social tirade and told reporters he is 'absolutely' considering NATO withdrawal and voices 'disgust' with the alliance. Rutte told CNN Trump was 'clearly disappointed' with allies — 'I can see his point.' Former NATO Ambassador Daalder called it 'the worst crisis the alliance has ever experienced.' Washington Post: 'Trump vents at NATO but avoids rupture.' Euronews: 'NATO holds' — but barely. Bloomberg: 'As Trump Bullies NATO, Europeans Question Its Deferential Chief.' Next test: NATO Foreign Ministers in Helsingborg, May 21-22.
footerNote: Data sourced from NATO HQ, White House, US Department of Defense, Reuters, Bloomberg, Wall Street Journal, The Economist, The Telegraph, TIME, Politico, German Federal Foreign Office, Axios, NPR, Washington Post, Al Jazeera, EU News, Swedish MFA, France 24, Euronews, CNN, PBS NewsHour
